tasks.title: Minimalist monolith source boundaries
tasks.objective: Make the app structure small, explicit, and understandable without introducing a framework-heavy architecture.
non_goals: Do not introduce TCA, Clean Architecture layers, dependency-injection frameworks, or protocol-heavy abstractions.
Do not remove monetization, shelf usability, StoreKit, or the core conversion primitive.
acceptance: Target source layout is documented.
Duplicate or speculative entry points are identified or removed.
Views call concrete services instead of owning conversion, StoreKit, Python, diagnostics, or file-access logic.
Any new dependency, entitlement, process boundary, or release hook has an ADR.

tasks.title: Python runtime isolation and bridge contract
tasks.objective: Ensure Python conversion cannot freeze or crash the Swift app process and has one typed bridge contract.
non_goals: Do not add cloud conversion.
Do not add a hard five-minute conversion timeout.
Do not broaden network access during conversion.
acceptance: All Python calls are behind one readiness-gated bridge API.
Bridge failures map to typed Swift errors.
Python access is serialized or moved behind a signed helper/XPC process.
Packaged Python import smoke passes from the built app.

tasks.title: Conversion job queue, state, and liveness
tasks.objective: Replace singleton conversion result behavior with per-job state, progress, cancellation, retry, and no-progress detection.
non_goals: Do not change pricing or product IDs.
Do not introduce batch conversion UX beyond the queue needed for correctness.
Do not use elapsed time alone as stalled-conversion detection.
acceptance: Each conversion has a job ID, stage, progress heartbeat, result, error, and cancellation state.
Two queued conversions cannot overwrite each other.
Cancel and retry are represented in service state and UI.
Tests cover success, failure, cancellation, and no-progress classification.

tasks.title: Offline model integrity
tasks.objective: Make conversion offline by default and treat missing, partial, corrupt, or unexpected model directories as unavailable.
non_goals: Do not download models implicitly during conversion.
Do not trust nonempty model directories as valid.
Do not use mutable model revisions for release builds.
acceptance: Conversion runtime sets offline environment variables.
Model downloads use staging, manifest validation, and atomic promotion.
Validated manifests include repo, revision, expected files, and checksums where practical.
Offline smoke fails clearly when required models are unavailable.

tasks.title: Native metadata extraction and sandbox temp safety
tasks.objective: Replace external metadata tools with Apple APIs and remove broad temporary-directory sandbox exceptions.
non_goals: Do not depend on PATH tools such as exiftool, ffprobe, or ffmpeg.
Do not keep /private/tmp sandbox exceptions.
Do not widen Python file access roots.
acceptance: Image metadata uses ImageIO/CoreGraphics.
Media metadata uses AVFoundation.
Python TMPDIR points to an app-owned per-job workspace.
Entitlement check fails on temporary absolute-path exceptions.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - Native Apple Replacements and Sandbox Safety

tasks.title: Effective plist, entitlements, and Quick Action handoff
tasks.objective: Make App Store metadata, services, URL schemes, speech permissions, app groups, and Quick Action handoff valid in the built app.
non_goals: Do not trust arbitrary custom-URL file paths.
Do not add new entitlements without an ADR.
Do not require Apple account credentials for local checks.
acceptance: Effective built Info.plist contains required URL, Services, and speech keys.
App Group identifiers use registered group-style names.
Quick Action handoff uses App Group storage plus copied files or security-scoped bookmarks.
CI checks plist and source entitlements.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - App Store Metadata and Entitlements

tasks.title: Consumable pack accounting review
tasks.objective: Ensure paid document pack credits are not client-authoritative in a way that creates support, refund, or review risk.
non_goals: Do not remove monetization or the paid conversion primitive.
Do not add a server unless the ADR proves it is necessary.
Do not change product IDs without App Store review notes.
acceptance: Pack credit accounting has an ADR.
Refund/revocation/interrupted purchase behavior is tested or explicitly blocked from release.
StoreKit restore and transaction listener behavior is covered.
User-facing purchase failure messages are recoverable.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - App Store Metadata and Entitlements

tasks.title: Observability, diagnostics, and fault injection
tasks.objective: Replace ad hoc diagnostics with OSLog, signposts, correlation IDs, redacted bundles, and failure-mode tests.
non_goals: Do not collect automatic telemetry.
Do not log document contents, extracted Markdown, passwords, or full local paths.
Do not add third-party crash analytics before Apple-native crash triage is proven insufficient.
acceptance: Core services use OSLog categories and per-job correlation IDs.
Conversion stages have signposts or equivalent structured stage logs.
Fault-injection tests cover bridge failure, stalled conversion, temp cleanup, partial model download, corrupt files, and huge files.
Diagnostic bundle generation is redacted and user-approved.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - Observability, CI, and Release Validation

tasks.title: Crash and bug reporting runbook
tasks.objective: Define and implement the privacy-first support path for crashes, conversion failures, model failures, and StoreKit issues.
non_goals: Do not silently upload diagnostics.
Do not include source documents or extracted text in support bundles.
Do not add Sentry or another service unless Apple-native crash reporting is documented as insufficient.
acceptance: Incident runbook exists for crash spikes, helper crashes, corrupt model rollouts, feature flag mistakes, and StoreKit failures.
Help > Report a Problem flow is specified or implemented.
User can preview and omit diagnostics before sending.
GitHub/support templates match the runbook categories.

tasks.title: Release dependency lock and upstream intake
tasks.objective: Make upstream adoption explicit, reproducible, and gated by local validation instead of ad hoc package upgrades.
non_goals: Do not auto-upgrade dependencies from scheduled jobs.
Do not adopt upstream conversion changes without a corpus case.
Do not accept local upstream patches without an ADR and removal condition.
acceptance: Runtime dependencies are exactly pinned for release.
Current, candidate, and latest-upstream states are documented and enforced by scripts.
Scheduled upstream watch creates/updates a tracking issue.
Upstream candidates require URL, local reproduction/corpus case, impact, security review, and rollback plan.
Conversion-quality upstream changes require corpus fixture or benchmark coverage before adoption.
Local patches to upstream behavior require ADRs with removal conditions and packaged-app validation.
Forks and cherry-picks are tracked as temporary candidates pinned to immutable commits or packaged artifacts with upstream PR context and rollback plans.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - Release Engineering and Upstream Validation

tasks.title: Agent task automation and release hooks
tasks.objective: Make multi-agent task tracking executable through a validated registry and GitHub issue/label sync.
non_goals: Do not replace the implementation plan.
Do not create duplicate issues for the same task.
Do not mark a task complete without validation evidence.
acceptance: Task registry validates locally and in CI.
Labels can be created or updated from the registry.
GitHub issues can be created or updated from the registry using the GitHub CLI.
Implementation plan is updated when P0 scope changes.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - Agent Task Tracking and Pipeline Hooks

tasks.title: Minimalist core conversion rewrite
tasks.objective: Replace the fragile singleton conversion path with ConversionQueue, ConversionRunner, and PythonWorker while keeping the existing UI and monetization intact.
non_goals: Do not introduce TCA, heavyweight MVVM frameworks, enterprise layers, protocol forests, or speculative abstractions.
Do not redesign the shelf, paywall, product IDs, pricing, or App Store business model.
Do not move Python to XPC in this task unless the small worker boundary is already passing; deeper helper isolation remains P0-002.
Do not add batch conversion features beyond the queue correctness required for one source of truth.
acceptance: Add Domain/ConversionJob.swift, Domain/ConversionResult.swift, Domain/ConversionError.swift, and Domain/Entitlement.swift or document why any one remains unnecessary.
Add @MainActor ConversionQueue with jobs, add(url:), cancel(id:), retry(id:), and one source of truth for progress/result/error state.
Add ConversionRunner with run(_:) async -> ConversionResult and no UI ownership.
Add PythonWorker so only PythonWorker or the lower-level bootstrap bridge imports PythonKit for conversion/model Python calls.
Deprecate or remove ConversionService singleton result polling from shelf, intents, and views.
Views render queue state and send actions; they do not poll global conversion result or copy files for conversion.
Architecture boundary CI enforces no PythonKit imports outside the approved worker/bootstrap boundary and no AppKit file/pasteboard mechanics in views.
Tests cover two queued jobs, cancellation, retry, Python error mapping, temp cleanup, and no-progress classification without a hard five-minute timeout.

tasks.title: Apple storage semantics and user-facing abstraction
tasks.objective: Make document access reliable across Apple storage locations while keeping implementation toolkit details out of normal user-facing copy.
non_goals: Do not add cloud conversion or iCloud document storage as a product feature.
Do not expose internal converter/toolkit names in user-facing UI, errors, onboarding, paywall, App Store copy, or support copy except licenses and explicit diagnostic previews.
Do not add a new architecture layer; keep storage handling behind concrete file/storage services.
acceptance: iCloud Drive, Desktop/Documents-in-iCloud, external drives, network volumes, File Provider locations, read-only sources, and denied save locations are validated.
Delayed conversion and Quick Action handoff use security-scoped access/bookmarks or copied app-owned files instead of raw paths.
Evicted or unavailable cloud-backed files fail with actionable product-level errors.
Release validation includes storage scenario checks and a user-facing copy audit for implementation-detail leaks.
Any Apple-native-only extraction mode is documented as a deferred product option unless explicitly promoted to v1 scope.
tasks.release_gate: P0 - Apple Storage and User-Facing Abstraction
